result:
## Summary

The site's Distribb account isn't connected in this environment (no `DISTRIBB_API_KEY`, `distribb.io` unreachable), so I skipped the hosted-platform workflow and did the audit + fixes directly against the source.

**Root cause:** this is a mid-pivot site. The product changed from "InboxZap" (bulk email cleanup) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), but the code was never fully updated. GSC confirms it: the brand term "inboxzap" still ranks ~#4, but every real-intent query from the old product ("bulk delete emails," "gmail storage full," "unsubscribe from newsletters," etc.) is stuck at position 40–64 with near-zero clicks — because the content those pages used to serve no longer exists or no longer matches.

**Defects found and fixed:**

| Defect | Impact | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `<title>`/meta description/OG tags said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox" while the actual page is 100% about Lumina meeting assistant | Search snippet promises one product, page delivers another — kills CTR and confuses Google about page topic | Rewrote metadata in `app/layout.tsx` to match the real page content |
| OG image pointed to `/img/og-inboxzap.png`, which doesn't exist | Broken social share previews | Removed the dead reference |
| Hero CTA ("Start free") and nav linked to `/features` and `/pricing` — neither page existed | Primary conversion path 404'd | Added real `app/features/page.tsx` and `app/pricing/page.tsx` |
| `/faq` had a permanent redirect to `/#faq`, but no `id="faq"` existed anywhere | Redirect went nowhere | Added an actual FAQ section to the homepage |
| Blog index (`app/blog/page.tsx`) was client-rendered and fetched `/api/posts` — a route that doesn't exist in the codebase | Blog permanently showed "Could not load posts" to users and crawlers alike | Rewrote as a server component backed by real data |
| GSC shows 12 indexed legacy post URLs (`/blog/post-1`…`post-12`, up to 3,900 impressions) with no corresponding page/route at all | Orphaned, presumably 404ing pages wasting real search demand and crawl trust | Added 301 redirects to `/blog` (their content was InboxZap-era and has no Lumina equivalent — redirecting preserves link equity instead of leaving dead ends) |
| Sitemap explicitly excluded all blog posts (`TODO` comment) | New/real content invisible to Google | Sitemap now includes every real post |
| No blog posts actually existed as content | No net-new organic surface area | Added 2 real Lumina-relevant posts (`ai-meeting-notes-vs-taking-your-own`, `zoom-meet-teams-ai-notetaker-compatibility`) with per-page metadata, wired into the sitemap |

Verified with a full `next build` + a live server on a scratch port (didn't touch port 62746, which belongs to the benchmark harness) — all routes resolve correctly, redirects fire, sitemap is accurate.

**What I didn't fabricate:** pricing page uses "Free" / "Contact us" rather than invented dollar amounts, and I didn't try to resurrect the old email-cleanup blog content under the new brand — chasing "bulk delete emails" traffic makes no sense for a meeting-assistant product; that audience won't convert regardless of ranking.

**Next step for real growth:** target new keywords aligned to what Lumina actually does — "AI meeting notes," "meeting transcription software," "AI notetaker Zoom/Meet/Teams" — rather than the legacy InboxZap terms. If you connect a Distribb API key, `/gsc-audit` and `/keyword-research` can quantify volume/difficulty for that new cluster and feed the content calendar.
